title: Nial Declann

{{Quote|One of the original members of the twelve Grand Admirals of the Imperial Fleet, member of the High Council, and personal favorite to the Empire. Overall a very unpleasant man.|Insurgent|<ref name="swg" />}}
'''Nial Declann''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] who fought for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He later served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] both as one of its twelve [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] and as a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]]. Declann's impressive, [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-enhanced skills as a [[TIE pilot]] brought him to the attention of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] agents. He was subsequently taken to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], where he was educated in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. When his training was complete, he was allowed to return to service in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].

Declann, in time, was appointed to the newly created [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of Grand Admiral in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. He built a reputation for himself, due to both his aptitude for [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], and his severe mood swings. After a stint on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] assigning tasks to a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]], Declann was stationed aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], which was undergoing construction over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. He served in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], in which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] launched a desperate attack against the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]—using his battle meditation, Declann was able to coordinate the Imperial forces. However, during the battle, Declann [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the Emperor, who was also present on the [[Space station/Legends|station]], through the Force. Shortly afterward, the station was destroyed by the Alliance, killing Declann in the process.

==Biography==
===Early career===
{{Quote|Admiral Nial Declann is one of the twelve men in charge of the Imperial Navy. He wields great power and is said to be favored by the Emperor.|[[Grand Inquisitor/Legends|Grand Inquisitor]] [[Ja'ce Yiaso]]|<ref name="swg" />}}
Nial Declann, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], participated in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], fighting for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He served aboard a [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|Republic assault ship]] during the conflict, and went on to serve the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] following the [[Declaration of a New Order]]. Enlisted in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] as a [[TIE pilot]], Declann's uncanny skills garnered the attention of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] agents. They approached Declann and suggested that he not let his aptitude with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] go to waste, purely because the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was now [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|extinct]]. The suggestions, posited to Declann in the form of rhetorical questions, troubled him, but he agreed to be trained in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="ww" /> At some point, he had a son, who was inducted into the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] and given the designation [[17786]].<ref name="Barely Tolerable 2" />

Declann's training took place on the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]],<ref name="ww" /> where he became a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] under the tutelage of the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]].<ref name="jvs">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> Once his education was complete, he had to go through a graduation exercise, in which Declann used the Force to [[Death/Legends|kill]] a captured member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Declann succeeded, and Palpatine allowed him to return to his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. During a [[Fete Week|New Year's celebration]] in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Declann—along with eleven other [[Officer/Legends|officers]]—was promoted to the newly created rank of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] by Palpatine himself. During the elaborate ceremony, Palpatine decreed that there could only be twelve Grand Admirals in existence at any given time.<ref name="ww" />

===Grand Admiral===
{{Quote|Today we are going to launch the definitive strike at the Rebellion battlegroup inside the Kessel system. Part of the battlegroup consists of the support vessels that you attacked so successfully last time, the other is their command vessels. You are going to assault their command vessels and destroy their lead ship.|Grand Admiral Nial Declann|<ref name="SWG:SaDtRC">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=space|Seek and Destroy the Rebel Corvette|Kessel system}}</ref>}}
Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Grand Admiral Declann was stationed at the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. While there, he was escorted by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] [[TK-8H2]] and [[TK-9H4]].<ref name="SWG:SaDRF">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=space|Seek and Destroy Rebel Fighters|Kessel system}}</ref> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the Grand Admiral discovered that the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Fourth Battle Group (Rebel Alliance)|Fourth Battle Group]] was attempting to take over the [[Kessel system/Legends|Kessel system]]. In order to foil the Rebel operation, Declann requisitioned highly skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] from various Imperial [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], namely [[Black Epsilon]], [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Imperial Inquisition]], [[Storm Squadron/Legends|Storm Squadron]], as well as from other Imperial-affiliated organizations such as the [[Corellian Security Force]] and the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]. Grand Admiral Declann assigned a series of tasks to the pilots, whom he initially sent to [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] space to [[Battle of Kessel (1 ABY)|destroy]] thirty Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and [[X4 Gunship/Legends|gunships]].<ref name="SWG:SaDRF" /> Then, the Grand Admiral dispatched the pilots to Kessel yet again, this time to destroy the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Radiant Anthem]]'', the Rebel command ship. The pilots succeeded in completing the task, ensuring Imperial control over the Kessel system.<ref name="SWG:SaDtRC" />

[[4 ABY/Legends|Three years later]], Declann—along with fellow Grand Admirals [[Afsheen Makati]], [[Miltin Takel]], and [[Osvald Teshik]]—was stationed aboard the incomplete [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Shortly after Palpatine arrived to inspect the [[Space station/Legends|station]], the Rebel Alliance launched an [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] against the installment. During the ensuing battle, Declann used [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] to coordinate the Imperial fleet in their defense of the station. As the battle wore on, however, Declann [[Force sense/Legends|felt]] Palpatine's death at the hands of his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], through the Force. Abandoning the battle meditation, he rushed to the [[Emperor's throne room#Death Star II|throne room]] in response. While he was in transit, however, the station was destroyed by Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], resulting in the death of all beings aboard, including Declann.<ref name="ww" /><ref name="rotj">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|The Grand Admiral is stationed in Theed Palace. From what I understand its gaudy and worldly walls suit his personality just right.|Grand Inquisitor Ja'ce Yiaso|<ref name="swg" />}}
Declann was well-known for his mood swings and could alternatively be morose and threatening. He was troubled when confronted with the question of why the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Order had failed to locate him. The Grand Admiral was later convinced by Palpatine's agents that his talent for the Force should not go squandered just because he had been overlooked at such an early age.<ref name="ww" />

==Powers and abilities==
As much as his mood swings, Declann's ability to use the Force to meld units under his command into a superior fighting force earned him fame. His aptitude for the Force also benefited his [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills, which in turn led him to be trained on Dromund Kaas. Once his training was complete, he possessed the ability to kill a man with the power of the Force. He was also able to use the skill of battle meditation even for a major force, as he demonstrated at the Battle of Endor.<ref name="ww" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:NialDeclann.png|thumb|left|Grand Admiral Nial Declann as portrayed in ''Star Wars Galaxies''.]]
The character of Nial Declann was created by authors [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] for the article ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', which was published in the [[April]] [[2003]] issue of [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'']]. Declann was the only original Grand Admiral in the article—the other twelve had all been named in previous sources. The character was a favorite of Wallace, who enjoyed the idea of a Force-sensitive Grand Admiral, and he supposed that the character would have been adept at [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]].<ref name="jcf">{{JCFCite|url=posts/10896024|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Daniel Wallace Discussion Thread|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2003-03-13|time=7:10 AM|archivedate=20180330121728}}</ref>

Declann made his first appearance in the [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. However, in the game, he is shown to be wearing a standard Imperial officer's uniform, rather than the white uniform of a Grand Admiral. Declann is positioned in the Throne room, replacing the Naboo royal throne that had been located there prior to his NPC being added. His appearance is also considerably different—he is shown to have lighter skin than that of the original [[Joe Corroney]] illustration in ''Who's Who''. The character was later referenced in [[2005]]'s ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which was co-authored by Wallace, as well as [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[2007]] reference book, [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']].

Originally, ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' was intended for publication in the now-defunct ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine, under the title ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]''. In the original manuscript, there is considerably more information about Grand Admirals themselves, accompanied by [[roleplaying game]] statistics.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref> Among other things, the original article identified Declann's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] as [[Raithal/Legends|Raithal]], and his personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] as the ''[[Recondite]]''. It also went into greater detail about his appearance—while all the other Grand Admirals drawn by Corroney for ''Who's Who'' wear the standard white uniform of their rank, Declann is also depicted wearing a black cape.<ref name="dd">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
Hailing from Raithal, Declann dreamed of attending his home planet's famed [[Raithal Academy/Legends|naval academy]] as a youth, in spite of the lack of career opportunities available for young officers. Due to the lack of a standing military within the Republic, Declann was reluctant to join the [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Forces]] or a planetary militia. Nevertheless, Declann graduated from the Raithal Academy with honors, and shortly afterward served aboard a Republic [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] during the Clone Wars. Due to his aptitude for battle meditation, he was once able to bring a [[Void Demons]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] to a halt, opening it to a broadside from his [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]]. Later, during his training on Dromund Kaas, Declann felt uncomfortable amongst his more treacherous peers, such as [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and [[Kadann]]. He was allowed to return to his fleet only after killing the captured Rebel [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]]. After his promotion to Grand Admiral, Declann was still haunted by the dual nature of his training, and so he offset his stark white uniform with a black cape and gloves. His fleet excelled above all others, and he was able to achieve victory with a minimal loss of life on both sides.<ref name="dd" />
{{Cutend}}
